entities_auto.title: Bayrol controller
entities_auto.description: Live readings and setpoints from the Bayrol bridge. Probe values for pH/Redox/Temperature — prefer the gated variant if available (filters out values while pump is off). Setpoints + alerts replace the previously manually configured Pool-Advisor targets — they stay in sync with what the controller is actually doing.

entities_manual.title: Manual measurements
entities_manual.description: Spot checks from a photometer or test strips (e.g. PoolLab). All optional. The manual pH is used to cross-check the controller's electrode.

targets.title: Targets & tolerances
targets.description: Defaults are common pool-chemistry guidelines. Blocks are collapsible.

ph.name: pH
ph.description: Acid-base value (scale 0–14, neutral 7). Target and critical thresholds come live from the Bayrol controller (see Bayrol controller section). Here only the normal tolerance band.

alkalinity.name: Alkalinity (TA)
alkalinity.description: Carbonate hardness (HCO₃⁻) — the chemical memory of pH. Plenty of TA = pH is robust against acid/base shocks; too little = every small dose makes pH jump. Too much TA absorbs too much acid, pH drifts up and scale forms. Raise with baking soda (NaHCO₃); lowering is a multi-day process (drop pH to ~7.0 and aerate heavily). Target 80–120 mg/l.

cyanuric.name: Cyanuric acid
cyanuric.description: Stabilizer — reversibly binds chlorine to protect it from UV breakdown. Without CYA, FC in direct sun halves in ~30–60 minutes; too much CYA instead binds chlorine so tightly it barely sanitises anymore (see FC/CYA ratio below). Does not degrade — can only be reduced by partial water exchange, so dose sparingly. Target 30–50 mg/l.

free_chlorine.name: Free chlorine
free_chlorine.description:
Primary sanitiser in the water — kills bacteria, viruses and algae via hypochlorous acid (HOCl). Too low and germs outrun chlorine (cloudiness, algae); too high irritates eyes and skin. The effective fraction depends strongly on CYA — TFP rule of thumb: target FC ≈ CYA × 0.075, e.g. 3 mg/l at CYA 40. The dosing controller regulates FC indirectly via redox.

Defaults are TFP-aligned for a reference CYA of 30 (min 1.5 / target 2.25 / max 4.5). When your CYA is measured and fresh, the advisor automatically raises these values to match your actual CYA level. If CYA is missing or stale, the values configured here act as fallback.

data.fc_critical_high: Free Cl critical high (mg/l, swim sperre)

combined_chlorine.name: Combined chlorine
combined_chlorine.description: Spent chlorine compounds (chloramines) — formed by reaction of free chlorine with skin, hair, sweat, urine and organic debris. The typical "chlorine smell" and irritated eyes come from CC, not from active chlorine (a properly run pool has no smell). Ideal value is 0; if CC stays persistently above the watch threshold, only breakpoint chlorination helps — push FC briefly to 10× CC to chemically break all chloramines apart.

redox.name: Redox (ORP)
redox.description: Oxidation potential in mV. Target and alert thresholds come live from the Bayrol controller. Here only the normal tolerance band.

drift.name: Probe drift
drift.description: Dosing-controller electrodes age and drift. The advisor compares the pH probe against the photometer and the redox probe against a chemically-derived expected value. Above threshold → recalibrate.

stale.name: Stale thresholds
stale.description: PoolLab values are read live — if you haven't measured for too long, the last value loses meaning. Per-parameter rhythms: TA/CYA stable (rare ok), FC dynamic (more often).

chemicals_auto.title: Chemicals — dosing system
chemicals_auto.description: Products your dosing system (e.g. Bayrol) continuously dispenses. Consumption tracked automatically via pump_state — no confirmation buttons needed.

routine_ph_minus.name: Routine pH-Minus
routine_ph_minus.description: Acid product in dosing canister (typically liquid HCl 14.9% or dry acid)

routine_cl.name: Routine chlorine
routine_cl.description: Chlorine product in dosing canister (typically liquid NaOCl) — optional

chemicals_manual.title: Chemicals — manual dosing
chemicals_manual.description: Products you dose yourself when the advisor recommends it. Pool-Advisor exposes a confirmation button per chemistry. All sections optional — leave empty if not used.

ph_minus_manual.name: Manual pH-Minus
ph_minus_manual.description: Alternative to dosing-system acid for ad-hoc manual events (typically dry acid granulate, NaHSO4)

ph_plus.name: pH-Plus
ph_plus.description: Base to raise pH (Soda Ash, Na2CO3)

shock.name: Manual chlorine
shock.description: For breakpoint/algae or manual top-up — granulate (dichlor/Chlorifix) or Cal-Hypo

testmodus.title: Test mode
testmodus.description: When active, the advisor uses these static values instead of real entity readings. Perfect for walking through workflows and verifying recommendations. All values optional — empty = not measured.

init.title: Pool Advisor — Edit settings
init.description: Which section do you want to edit?
